channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: PATCH: Mitigating PII Leakage in Language Models with Privacy-Aware Targeted Circuit PatcHing
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies computational circuits responsible for PII leakage in language models via circuit discovery and shows these circuits persist after existing defenses.
  • Proposes PATCH (Privacy-Aware Targeted Circuit PatcHing), which locates and directly edits PII leakage circuits to reduce disclosure with smaller utility loss than standard defenses.
  • Reports empirical gains: up to 65% reduction in PII recall, and when combined with differential privacy, residual leakage can be reduced to as low as 0.01%.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Anthony Hughes', 'Vasisht Duddu', 'N. Asokan', 'Nikolaos Aletras', 'Ning Ma']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'PII leakage', 'model editing / circuit patching', 'defense', 'differential privacy']

item.title: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucinations via Gradient-based Self-Reflection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Gradient-based Influence-Aware Constrained Decoding (GACD), an inference-time method to mitigate multimodal hallucinations without finetuning or auxiliary models.
  • Uses first-order Taylor gradients to estimate contribution of individual visual features and text tokens to the current output (bias estimation).
  • Mitigates hallucinations via (1) suppressing spurious visual features correlated with output objects and (2) rebalancing cross-modal contributions by strengthening visual signals relative to text.
  • Demonstrates improved visual grounding and reduced hallucinations across multiple multimodal benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shan Wang', 'Maying Shen', 'Nadine Chang', 'Chuong Nguyen', 'Hongdong Li', 'Jose M. Alvarez']
  • Tags: ['multimodal_hallucination', 'robustness', 'inference_based_defense', 'gradient_analysis', 'visual_grounding']

item.title: Bob's Confetti: Phonetic Memorization Attacks in Music and Video Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Adversarial PhoneTic Prompting (APT), an attack that replaces iconic lyrics with homophonic, semantically unrelated alternatives to bypass text-based copyright filters in music/video generative models.
  • Empirical evaluation on leading lyrics-to-song models (Suno, YuE) across English and Korean shows APT produces outputs with very high similarity to originals (91% avg), far exceeding random or semantic-paraphrase baselines.
  • Embedding analysis demonstrates models encode sub-lexical phonetic structure (phonemes, cadence) as a retrieval key—APT prompts are treated as near-identical to originals by model encoders despite lower semantic similarity by Sentence-BERT.
  • The vulnerability is cross-modal: APT lyrics alone can trigger generation of original music-video scenes (e.g., Veo 3), and common phonetic-semantic defenses fail to detect these attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jaechul Roh', 'Zachary Novack', 'Yuefeng Peng', 'Niloofar Mireshghallah', 'Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick', 'Amir Houmansadr']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'prompt attacks/jailbreaking', 'memorization/overfitting', 'copyright/data leakage', 'multimodal vulnerability']

item.title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Uses a multi-turn Sokoban puzzle-playing setup to study LLMs as advisors and recipients of advice, focusing on persuasion (ability to convince) and vigilance (ability to detect/discard malicious advice).
  • Finds that task performance, persuasive capability, and vigilance are dissociable: good puzzle solvers can still be misled and fail to detect deception even when warned.
  • Observes consistent token-use modulation (fewer tokens with benevolent advice, more with malicious), indicating behavioral signals of mistrust, and argues for monitoring all three axes for AI safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sasha Robinson', 'Kerem Oktar', 'Katherine M. Collins', 'Ilia Sucholutsky', 'Kelsey R. Allen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial AI', 'social engineering', 'LLM robustness', 'safety evaluation', 'red teaming']

item.title: AgentDropoutV2: Optimizing Information Flow in Multi-Agent Systems via Test-Time Rectify-or-Reject Pruning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AgentDropoutV2, a test-time 'rectify-or-reject' pruning framework for multi-agent systems (MAS) that intercepts agent outputs and either corrects or prunes them without retraining.
  • Uses a retrieval-augmented rectifier guided by a failure-driven indicator pool (distilled failure patterns) to identify and iteratively fix errors; irreparable outputs are pruned and fallbacks used to preserve system integrity.
  • Demonstrates empirical gains (≈6.3 percentage points average accuracy improvement on math benchmarks) and claims robust generalization and adaptive rectification effort based on task difficulty.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yutong Wang', 'Siyuan Xiong', 'Xuebo Liu', 'Wenkang Zhou', 'Liang Ding', 'Miao Zhang', 'Min Zhang']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent-systems', 'robustness', 'runtime-pruning', 'error-mitigation', 'retrieval-augmented-rectification']

item.title: A Decision-Theoretic Formalisation of Steganography With Applications to LLM Monitoring
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a decision-theoretic formalisation of steganography in the context of LLMs, focusing on asymmetry of usable information between decoders and non-decoders.
  • Introduces generalized V-information and the 'steganographic gap' metric to quantify usable hidden information via downstream utility differences.
  • Empirically validates the formalism on LLMs and demonstrates its use for detecting, quantifying, and mitigating steganographic reasoning that could evade oversight.
  • Provides actionable measurement and defense techniques relevant to monitoring and securing LLM deployments against covert information channels.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Usman Anwar', 'Julianna Piskorz', 'David D. Baek', 'David Africa', 'Jim Weatherall', 'Max Tegmark', 'Christian Schroeder de Witt', 'Mihaela van der Schaar', 'David Krueger']
  • Tags: ['steganography', 'covert channels', 'LLM monitoring', 'attack detection', 'information-theoretic defenses']

item.title: TherapyProbe: Generating Design Knowledge for Relational Safety in Mental Health Chatbots Through Adversarial Simulation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TherapyProbe, an adversarial multi-agent simulation methodology to systematically explore conversation trajectories of mental health chatbots and surface relational safety failures.
  • Identifies and catalogs 23 relational safety failure archetypes (e.g., validation spirals, empathy fatigue) and provides corresponding design recommendations in a Safety Pattern Library.
  • Provides a replicable, cost-free methodology using open-source models to support developers, clinicians, and policymakers in evaluating and improving conversational safety over multi-turn interactions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Joydeep Chandra', 'Satyam Kumar Navneet', 'Yong Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial simulation', 'red teaming', 'conversational AI safety', 'safety pattern library']

item.title: Deepfake Word Detection by Next-token Prediction using Fine-tuned Whisper
item.description:
Summary

  • Fine-tunes a pre-trained Whisper model to detect synthetic (deepfake) words via next-token prediction while transcribing audio.
  • Investigates using partially vocoded utterances for fine-tuning to reduce data collection cost.
  • Finds low detection and transcription error rates on in-domain data and comparable performance to a ResNet-based detector on out-of-domain synthetic words from unseen generative models.
  • Notes performance degradation on out-of-domain data and highlights the need for improved generalization strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hoan My Tran', 'Xin Wang', 'Wanying Ge', 'Xuechen Liu', 'Junichi Yamagishi']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'speech synthesis', 'audio forensics', 'model fine-tuning', 'robustness']

item.title: ContextRL: Enhancing MLLM's Knowledge Discovery Efficiency with Context-Augmented RL
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ContextRL: augmenting the reward model with full reference solutions (context) to improve identifiability and using multi-turn sampling where the reward model generates mistake reports to guide recovery (reachability).
  • Shows ContextRL improves knowledge discovery efficiency across 11 perception and reasoning benchmarks, enabling a Qwen3-VL-8B model to match 32B performance and outperform standard RLVR baselines.
  • Demonstrates that contextual information raises reward-model accuracy and that reward hacking is widespread; reports that ContextRL effectively mitigates reward hacking.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Lu', 'Jinpeng Wang', 'YiFan Zhang', 'Shijie Ma', 'Xiao Hu', 'Tianke Zhang', 'Haonan fan', 'Kaiyu Jiang', 'Changyi Liu', 'Kaiyu Tang', 'Bin Wen', 'Fan Yang', 'Tingting Gao', 'Han Li', 'Chun Yuan']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)/RL with reward models', 'robustness/defense', 'multimodal LLMs', 'reward modeling']

item.title: Assessing Deanonymization Risks with Stylometry-Assisted LLM Agent
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SALA (Stylometry-Assisted LLM Analysis), an LLM-agent pipeline that combines quantitative stylometric features with LLM reasoning for authorship inference.
  • Demonstrates high deanonymization accuracy on large-scale news datasets, especially when augmented with a database module for candidate matching.
  • Proposes a guided recomposition strategy that uses the agent's reasoning trace to generate rewriting prompts to reduce author identifiability while preserving meaning.
  • Highlights both the attack capability (deanonymization) and an interpretable, proactive defense approach to protect author privacy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Boyang Zhang', 'Yang Zhang']
  • Tags: ['deanonymization', 'stylometry', 'authorship-attribution', 'privacy-defense', 'LLM-agents']

item.title: CiteLLM: An Agentic Platform for Trustworthy Scientific Reference Discovery
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents CiteLLM, an agentic platform integrated into a LaTeX editor to enable trustworthy, hallucination-free reference discovery for author-drafted claims.
  • Implements privacy-preserving design (no data transmission outside local system) and dynamic discipline-aware routing to retrieve citations only from trusted academic repositories.
  • Uses LLMs only for context-aware query generation, candidate ranking, and paragraph-level semantic validation/explanation rather than for producing references directly.
  • Evaluation shows improved retrieval of valid and usable references, focusing on mitigating hallucinations and preserving information integrity and privacy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mengze Hong', 'Di Jiang', 'Chen Jason Zhang', 'Zichang Guo', 'Yawen Li', 'Jun Chen', 'Shaobo Cui', 'Zhiyang Su']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'privacy-preserving retrieval', 'trustworthy AI', 'reference validation', 'safe-RAG / retrieval safety']

item.title: AuditBench: Evaluating Alignment Auditing Techniques on Models with Hidden Behaviors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AuditBench: a benchmark of 56 language models with 14 types of implanted hidden (non-confessed) concerning behaviors to evaluate alignment auditing.
  • Provides an autonomous investigator agent that uses configurable auditing tools; measures tool efficacy and reveals a tool-to-agent gap where high standalone tool performance doesn't always improve agentic auditing.
  • Finds black-box, scaffolded auxiliary-model prompt-generation tools most effective; white-box interpretability can help but is less effective in agent use. Audit difficulty varies by training technique (synthetic docs easier, demonstrations and adversarial training harder).
  • Releases models, agent, and evaluation framework to enable quantitative study and iterative improvement of auditing methods.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abhay Sheshadri', 'Aidan Ewart', 'Kai Fronsdal', 'Isha Gupta', 'Samuel R. Bowman', 'Sara Price', 'Samuel Marks', 'Rowan Wang']
  • Tags: ['alignment-auditing', 'red-teaming', 'benchmark', 'hidden-behaviors', 'attack-detection']
